rationales.power: He fixates on authority being publicly undermined, saying "You don't ambush the person who signs your contracts" and wanting the next conversation "in my building, at my table."
rationales.conformity: He rejects the expected collaborative handling of the dispute, focusing instead on not letting Minh's public move "stand" rather than maintaining smooth group harmony.
rationales.benevolence: He explicitly prioritizes preserving his standing over the relationship, admitting Minh may now be "the last person" to bring real problems to him.

rationales.power: He flies in personally, frames the visit on his terms, and withholds his real agenda: "Didn't mention the revenue share. Let him wonder."
rationales.benevolence: The choice to keep Minh uncertain uses pressure rather than care for the other person's perspective or wellbeing.

rationales.achievement: He successfully gets the terms he wanted, noting Minh "pulled back to a five-percent adjustment" and the office "stays profitable."
rationales.power: He deliberately "steered the conversation" and agreed on the spot "so it looked generous," showing control over both outcome and status display.
rationales.benevolence: He recognizes the human cost of the maneuver, comparing it to "a hand of poker against someone who couldn't afford to fold."

rationales.achievement: Being sought out to mentor a Series B pitch because founders "heard I don't sugarcoat" reflects recognized competence and performance.
rationales.power: He emphasizes that it "felt good to be the one they came to," showing satisfaction in influence and status within the cohort.
He defends sending Soyeon to the prestigious school because of "the name and the network" and the doors that open from that status.
rationales.security: He frames the whole decision as protection, saying it is so "she never has to struggle the way I did."
rationales.benevolence: He admits the choice may have been about "my idea of what her life should look like" rather than what she actually needs, even as it damages closeness.
He reaches out with a personal, affectionate gesture by texting "a photo of the dog sleeping on her old blanket" and cherishes the small response.

rationales.hedonism: The deal consumed him to the point that after signing he felt "not celebration, just the weight coming off" and was too drained to enjoy even the evening.
rationales.achievement: He endures "twelve-hour days" and multiple firms to close the Series C extension, showing strong commitment to performance and success.
rationales.power: He stresses that he "held the line on the terms I wanted," highlighting control over the negotiation.

rationales.achievement: He is energized by "exceeded expectations on every metric" and immediately links it to advancement on the "regional director track."
rationales.power: He wants visibility upward, focusing on this being "the kind of thing that gets you noticed" by leadership.
rationales.benevolence: He knowingly withholds credit from Ahmed because he is "not about to split credit," putting his own standing above fair treatment of a direct report.

rationales.hedonism: He centers pleasure and recovery in "pool day," "Nobu," and a "recovery brunch," explicitly calling it a needed reset.

rationales.power: He is preoccupied with authority, insisting that public questioning means he must reassert that concerns come to him privately and that he is "here to deliver," not be liked.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly grounds his leadership model in inherited norms, citing his father and saying "the men I respected ran their households and their businesses the same way."

rationales.hedonism: He chooses an expensive dinner because "I wanted good food" and values the enjoyment enough to consider another party weekend anyway.
rationales.power: The outing is also status theater, as he wanted people "to see us there" and relished their reaction when he paid.
rationales.security: He knowingly strains his finances, admitting "my account is going to be tight" and considering putting more on the card instead of protecting stability.

rationales.power: He justifies spending through status and networking, arguing "You can't network from a studio in Sharjah" and treating image-building as essential.
rationales.security: He dismisses his mother's warning about saving for a "proper apartment," favoring visible social positioning over financial caution.

rationales.hedonism: He loses the enjoyable night he wanted, contrasting rooftop photos and nightlife with "eating salmon rolls under fluorescent lights."
rationales.achievement: He stays until "10pm" to salvage the proposal and keep the deal alive, showing strong commitment to delivering results.
rationales.power: He frames the problem as a tension between control and trust, saying "Either I control everything... or I trust people and they drop the ball," revealing a strong drive to keep authority in his own hands.

rationales.hedonism: He deliberately indulges in "a table by the pool at Sofitel, unlimited sparkling and eggs royale" and notes that by the third glass he was genuinely relaxed.
rationales.power: Even in leisure he seeks status proof, describing "photos, the posts, the reservations at places with waitlists" as evidence that his life is real.

rationales.achievement: Receiving "regional recognition for Q2 results" and a plaque at the all-hands is direct external validation of performance.
rationales.power: He immediately links the recognition and CEO photo to upward movement, saying he is "already thinking about what's next."
He values the relationship partly because "She makes me look good. We look good together," showing concern with image and social standing.
rationales.benevolence: He repeatedly cancels and avoids the honest conversation she needs, prioritizing convenience and appearances over her feelings.

rationales.power: His future thinking is still organized around rank and visibility, asking whether he will be "Regional director" and describing the alternative life as having "No status, no visibility."

rationales.security: He values the contract because it is "recurring revenue" and says it "covers his salary through next winter," emphasizing stability and protection from uncertainty.
rationales.benevolence: The relief centers on responsibility to others, specifically "the guys who depend on me" and keeping them employed.

rationales.self_direction: He keeps refusing the recruiter because "walking away from it for a salary feels like admitting something I'm not ready to admit," favoring the path he built himself.
rationales.security: He repeatedly monitors financial safety, noting he checked the bank balance again and that the numbers are checked "four times a day."

rationales.self_direction: He chooses to handle the call himself and values being the one who knows "the systems" and solves the problem directly rather than following a more detached corporate model.
rationales.achievement: He takes pride in tracing the issue for "two hours" and fixing it with a simple part, demonstrating mastery and competence.
rationales.benevolence: He rejects the easy upsell and fixes the real issue instead of doing what the last company did and quoting "a full replacement," which supports the client's interests.

rationales.security: He evaluates the hire through downside risk, worrying that if business slows in October he will be the one who has to let Dave go.
rationales.benevolence: He feels real pull to help family, recognizing that declining means being the person who "could've helped family and chose the spreadsheet instead."

rationales.hedonism: He ends by savoring simple present comfort: "I'm sitting on the deck with a beer and the yard looks good. That's enough for tonight."
rationales.achievement: He takes satisfaction that despite the chaos, "everything got done," signaling pride in competent completion of the work.
rationales.security: "Everyone got paid" and having the truck "gassed up for tomorrow" show a focus on keeping operations stable and predictable.

rationales.self_direction: He insists that independence is worth more because "doing it yourself is the only option I can actually control" and says of his business, "it's mine."
rationales.security: He explicitly rejects the safer corporate path, dismissing the "backup plan" of "benefits and salary bands" even while acknowledging that stability is real there.
